data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: WhatsApp uses a microservices architecture to enhance scalability and manageability by breaking down applications into smaller, independent services.
data.technical_spec: Several self-contained services collaborating over APIs.

data.title: End-to-End Encryption Infrastructure
data.description: The architecture supports robust end-to-end encryption ensuring privacy and security of messages.
data.technical_spec: Uses Signal Protocol for encrypting messages peer-to-peer.

data.title: Distributed Database Systems
data.description: The use of distributed databases supports massive data storage and retrieval to handle millions of concurrent users.
data.technical_spec: Apache Cassandra or similar NoSQL databases.

data.title: Load Balancers
data.description: Load balancers are implemented to distribute network or application traffic efficiently across multiple servers.
data.technical_spec: Nginx or HAProxy for balancing incoming requests.

data.title: Real-Time Messaging
data.description: Supports real-time communication by instantly sending messages to users.
data.technical_spec: Websockets or proprietary protocol for persistent connections.
